hero.title: One YAML block, twelve targets, every release.
hero.sub: Reusable GitHub Actions workflows that test, cross-compile and release every Null-family Zig project. Maintainer infrastructure: nothing to install — a caller repo references the workflows and inherits the whole pipeline.

primary.label: Reference from a caller repo
primary.code:
jobs:
  ci:
    uses: nullclaw/nullbuilder/.github/workflows/zig-ci.yml@v1
    permissions:
      contents: read
    with:
      binary_name: nullclaw
      artifact_prefix: nullclaw

alts.label: Nightly pipeline
alts.code:
jobs:
  nightly:
    uses: nullclaw/nullbuilder/.github/workflows/zig-nightly.yml@v1
    permissions:
      actions: read
      contents: read
    with:
      binary_name: nullclaw
      artifact_prefix: nullclaw
      force: ${{ inputs.force || false }}

alts.label: Release pipeline
alts.code:
jobs:
  release:
    uses: nullclaw/nullbuilder/.github/workflows/zig-release.yml@v1
    permissions:
      contents: write
      packages: write
    secrets: inherit
    with:
      binary_name: nullclaw
      artifact_prefix: nullclaw
      publish_docker: true

quickstart:
There is nothing to install. In any Zig project on GitHub, add one workflow file that calls the shared pipeline:

```bash
# in your project repo
mkdir -p .github/workflows

# one job block wires up tests + cross-compiled ReleaseSmall builds
cat > .github/workflows/ci.yml <<'EOF'
name: CI
on: [push, pull_request]
jobs:
  ci:
    uses: nullclaw/nullbuilder/.github/workflows/zig-ci.yml@v1
    permissions:
      contents: read
    with:
      binary_name: myproject   # your binary under zig-out/bin
EOF

git add .github/workflows/ci.yml
git commit -m "ci: adopt nullbuilder shared workflows"
git push
```

features.title: One CI block
features.body: zig-ci.yml runs your tests, then cross-compiles ReleaseSmall binaries on a configurable target matrix. Zig caches and artifact upload included.

features.title: Twelve-target releases
features.body: Push a v* tag and zig-release.yml builds Linux, Android, macOS and Windows binaries, injects the version, and publishes a GitHub release with generated notes.

features.title: Nightlies that dedupe
features.body: zig-nightly.yml skips commits that already have a successful nightly, keeps artifacts 14 days, and can maintain a rolling prerelease.

features.title: Android without Gradle
features.body: aarch64, armv7 and x86_64 Android binaries cross-compiled with plain Zig plus an NDK sysroot libc file. No Java build system involved.

features.title: Hooks for odd builds
features.body: Setup, pre-test and pre-build commands, custom test command and build args, one e2e command pinned to a chosen target, optional Node.js with npm caching.

features.title: SHA-pinned supply chain
features.body: Every third-party action is pinned to a full commit SHA, with Dependabot updates. The helper actions are written in Zig and unit-tested with zig test.

note: Not an end-user tool, and no releases, tags or license file yet — callers pin to the v1 branch, and workflow inputs may change.
